rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MICHAEL MURRAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel Pltf To Provide Further Reesponses To Documents Requerst, Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 18, 2012, Line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT PARAIC O'DONOGHUE, DANIEL WALSH Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel Pltf To Provide Further Reesponses To Documents Requerst, Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the motion. All responses shall be delivered to movant's counsel by 1/15/13. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: LUIS ADOLFO MARQUEZ et al VS. PG & E et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Responses To Admission/Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 18, 2012, Line 7, Motion To Compel Responses To Admission/Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The court is not satisfied that the parties have exhausted their meet and confer obligations. The parties are ordered to further met and confer ( perhaps at 8:00 AM on 12/18) to attempt to limit and simplify the discovery requests at issue. The parties shall report at the hearing the progress they have made in condensing the discovery at issue. A ruling is deferred until the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: MARIE BACCHUS TAYLOR et al VS. DAVID SHEPARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 18, 2012, Line 15, CROSS DEFENDANT RAIZEL SYDELL ENTERPRISES LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Denied, without prejudice. The clerk properly entered default and struck Raizel Sydell Enterprises, LLC's Answer to the Cross-Complaint. "[A] plaintiff is entitled to the entry of default on the date he requests it if there is then no responsive pleading by the defendant on file; and that the plaintiff cannot be deprived of his right to the entry of a default merely because the clerk erroneously fails to perform his ministerial duty and does not enter it when requested." (Goddard v. Pollock (1974) 37 Cal.App.3d 137, 142.) Per the Register of Actions, on October 5, 2012, Mr. Reese submitted his request for entry of default. On October 18, 2012, Raizel Sydell Enterprises filed its Answer. (Motion, Liddicoat Decl., 2.) Mr. Liddicoat's declaration is insufficient to show attorney mistake, inadvertence or excusable neglect per Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b). (Motion, Liddicoat Decl., 3.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. DORA A. ROMERO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Set Aside And Vacate Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 18, 2012, Line 21, PLAINTIFF DISCOVER BANK'S Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Dismissal. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: WYDE VOICE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 18, 2012, Line 23, DEFENDANTS GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC., GOOGLE INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Judge Miller recused herself from this case. Per the Presiding Judge's Department, the motion is continued to January 14, 2013 at 1:30 pm, to be heard in Department 502 before Judge Robertson. The moving party shall provide a courtesy copy of its moving papers with Department 502 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date by December 31, 2012. The court has copies of the opposition and reply. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA SECURITY INSTITUTE, INC., A NEVADA et al VS. PRISCILLA CARLSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Reconsider Oct 18, 2012 Order Striking Labor Appear No Bond Posted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 18, 2012, Line 26, APPELLANTS CALIFORNIA SECURITY INSTITUTE, INC. and VANCE WHARTON (Amended) Motion To Reconsider Oct 18, 2012 Order Striking Labor Appeal No Bond Posted. Petitioner's motion to reconsider the Order Striking Labor Appeal issued on October 18, 2012 is granted. No opposition filed and Petitioner has shown good cause. Petitioner shall post an undertaking of $ 8,705.54 per the Labor Commission's modified final decision issued on October 10, 2012, no later than December 19, 2012 at 4:00 pm. (See Motion, Volkov Decl., 7, Exhibit E.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
